auto_network_tier.description: Specifies the network service tier for automatic allocations. Must be either 'PREMIUM' or 'STANDARD'.

drain_nat_ips.description: List of NAT IPs to be drained. Used for transitioning NAT IPs out of service.

enable_dynamic_port_allocation.description: Enables dynamic port allocation for NAT. If true, ports are assigned as needed.

enable_endpoint_independent_mapping.description: Enables endpoint-independent mapping for NAT, allowing bidirectional communication through NAT without restrictions.

endpoint_types.description: Defines the endpoint types for NAT. Accepted values include 'VM_PRIMARY_IP', 'VM_SECONDARY_IP'.

icmp_idle_timeout_sec.description: Timeout in seconds for idle ICMP connections. Must be a non-negative integer.

initial_nat_ips.description: List of static NAT IPs to be initially allocated. Must be valid external IPs.

log_config.description: Configuration for logging, including enablement and filter criteria.

max_ports_per_vm.description: Maximum number of ports allocated per VM. Must be a positive integer.

min_ports_per_vm.description: Minimum number of ports allocated per VM. Must be a positive integer and less than or equal to 'max_ports_per_vm'.

name.description: Name of the NAT resource. Must be unique within the project and adhere to naming conventions.

nat_ip_allocate_option.description: Specifies the allocation method for NAT IPs. Valid values are 'AUTO_ONLY' or 'MANUAL_ONLY'.

nat_ips.description: List of NAT IPs for manual allocation. Must be valid external IPs.

router.description: Name of the router that the NAT is associated with. Must already exist in the same region.

rules.description: List of NAT rules, including match conditions and actions for specific traffic flows.
rules.type: list(object({description: optional(string),match: string,rule_number: number,action: list(object({source_nat_active_ips: optional(list(string)),source_nat_drain_ips: optional(list(string))}))}))

source_subnetwork_ip_ranges_to_nat.description: Specifies which subnetwork IP ranges are NATed. Valid values are 'ALL_IP_RANGES', 'LIST_OF_SECONDARY_IP_RANGES'.

subnetwork.description: List of subnetworks to configure for NAT, including secondary IP ranges and source ranges.

tcp_established_idle_timeout_sec.description: Timeout in seconds for idle established TCP connections. Must be a non-negative integer.

tcp_time_wait_timeout_sec.description: Timeout in seconds for TCP connections in the TIME_WAIT state. Must be a non-negative integer.

tcp_transitory_idle_timeout_sec.description: Timeout in seconds for idle transitory TCP connections. Must be a non-negative integer.

timeouts.description: Specifies custom timeouts for create, update, or delete operations.

udp_idle_timeout_sec.description: Timeout in seconds for idle UDP connections. Must be a non-negative integer.
